nodes.title: Implementations Identify XanaNode Compatibility
nodes.summary: Implementations should identify themselves as XanaNode-compatible and reference the canonical specification without implying official ownership.

nodes.title: Protocol Docs Are Licensed CC-BY 4.0
nodes.summary: XanaNode protocol documentation is licensed under CC-BY 4.0 so the docs can be shared and adapted with attribution.

nodes.title: Relationships Preserve Knowledge
nodes.summary: Knowledge survives movement between tools when relationships carry meaning, evidence, authorship, and addressable context instead of collapsing into anonymous links.

nodes.title: Schemas, Validators, And Reference Code Are Apache-2.0
nodes.summary: XanaNode schemas, validators, tooling, and reference implementation code are licensed under Apache-2.0 for broad adoption.

nodes.title: XanaNode Name And Logo Are Trademarked
nodes.summary: The XanaNode name, logo, and related brand assets are governed as trademarks even when protocol docs and code are openly licensed.

nodes.title: XanaNode Contributors
nodes.summary: The people and future maintainers who author, review, preserve, and project XanaNode-compatible substrates.

nodes.title: Attribution License
nodes.summary: A license pattern that allows reuse while requiring credit to the original author or project.

nodes.title: Compatibility Attribution
nodes.summary: The convention that implementations should identify themselves as XanaNode-compatible and point to the canonical specification without implying ownership or endorsement.

nodes.title: Content License
nodes.summary: A license applied to authored text, documentation, examples, or explanatory material rather than executable reference code.

nodes.title: Knowledge Substrate
nodes.summary: The graph-shaped structure that lets information remain understandable across time by making claims, sources, media, fragments, and relationships explicit.

nodes.title: Licensing
nodes.summary: The legal layer that describes how documents, schemas, code, media, and names may be copied, reused, attributed, modified, or governed.

nodes.title: Open License
nodes.summary: A license that grants broad public permission to use, share, adapt, or implement a work while preserving stated conditions such as attribution or notices.

nodes.title: Permissive Open Source License
nodes.summary: An open source license family that allows broad reuse, modification, distribution, and commercial adoption with limited obligations.

nodes.title: Protocol Artifacts
nodes.summary: The portable JSON files, schemas, fragments, reports, and media references that allow tools to validate, compare, mount, import, merge, and render substrates.

nodes.title: Software License
nodes.summary: A license applied to source code, validators, tooling, schemas, and implementation artifacts.

nodes.title: Substrate Projection Layer
nodes.summary: A renderer or lens that presents a substrate for a particular use without becoming the substrate itself.

nodes.title: Trademark
nodes.summary: A protected name, logo, or mark that identifies the source and reputation of a project, product, organization, or community.

nodes.title: XanaNode
nodes.summary: A protocol for independently authored knowledge substrates that preserve relationships, provenance, lineage, disagreement, and addressable fragments.

nodes.title: What Is XanaNode?
nodes.summary: An explanation of XanaNode as a layered protocol, not a single viewer, website, repository, or graph visualization.

nodes.title: XanaNode Canonical Pack Bootstrap
nodes.summary: The point where Core begins shipping a mountable canonical XanaNode substrate pack as protocol JSON.

nodes.title: Relationships Preserve Knowledge Sentence
nodes.summary: Relationships preserve knowledge.

nodes.alt: A connected node mark with orange and blue paths crossing around a bright central node.

nodes.title: Markdown Recreation Friction
nodes.summary: Forcing authors to recreate validated protocol nodes as Markdown creates duplicate governance surfaces and loses the benefit of portable substrate artifacts.

nodes.title: Built By Bots
nodes.summary: The authoring and project identity associated with XanaNode development and media attribution.

nodes.title: Cytoscape Consortium
nodes.summary: The organization and community associated with Cytoscape and Cytoscape.js development.

nodes.title: Hugo Authors
nodes.summary: The open source project community responsible for Hugo.

nodes.title: Christian Siefen
nodes.summary: The personally identified author and maintainer of the canonical XanaNode substrate.

nodes.title: World Wide Web
nodes.summary: The public networked space where XanaNode domains, repositories, documentation, and published substrates are reached.

nodes.title: XanaNode Core SDK
nodes.summary: Renderer-independent parser, validator, graph builder, fragment engine, pack composer, suggestion engine, and exporter for XanaNode substrates.

nodes.title: XanaNode Hugo Theme
nodes.summary: Static-site renderer and graph viewer for XanaNode-compatible substrates.

nodes.title: XanaNode Protocol
nodes.summary: Canonical XanaNode protocol repository containing schemas, registries, specs, governance, examples, branding, and proposals.

nodes.title: XanaNode Studio
nodes.summary: Electron authoring workbench for creating nodes, editing protocol metadata, navigating graph context, and previewing projection layers.

nodes.title: XanaNode Workspace
nodes.summary: Local-first workspace engine for creating, opening, validating, building, importing, and snapshotting XanaNode substrates.

nodes.title: XNP-0004 Merge Validation
nodes.summary: A protocol proposal describing merge validation for independently authored substrates.

nodes.title: Protocol Implements Relationship Preservation
nodes.summary: A first-class relationship node representing that the XanaNode protocol implements the claim that relationships preserve knowledge.

nodes.title: Canonical Pack v0.1.0
nodes.summary: The first Core-bundled revision of the canonical XanaNode pack.

nodes.title: Canonical Schemas Registry

nodes.title: Claim
nodes.summary: A specific assertion that can be supported, disputed, revised, deprecated, or rejected.

nodes.title: Communication
nodes.summary: A first-class communication event or artifact whose topology, directionality, participants, and pattern affect how knowledge moved.

nodes.title: Community
nodes.summary: A group organized around an identity, platform, shared work, practice, interest, or tradition.

nodes.title: Concept
nodes.summary: An idea, pattern, theory, failure mode, or explanatory structure. A concept is built from claims.

nodes.title: Essay
nodes.summary: A human-authored argument that connects claims, sources, observations, and concepts.

nodes.title: Event
nodes.summary: Something that happened at a time and optionally at a place.

nodes.title: Fragment
nodes.summary: A stable, addressable portion of another node, source, media object, or publication used for deep linking, quotation, citation, transclusion, and revision-aware reference.

nodes.title: Hypothesis
nodes.summary: A provisional explanation, prediction, or model that can be tested, supported, falsified, revised, or abandoned.

nodes.title: Knowledge Gap
nodes.summary: A known absence, uncertainty, missing source, missing citation, lost document, unknown author, or unresolved area in a substrate.

nodes.title: Media
nodes.summary: An image, video, audio clip, screenshot, diagram, document, or other media item with provenance.

nodes.title: Observation
nodes.summary: A real-world case, symptom, example, incident, failure, or pattern being analyzed.

nodes.title: Organization
nodes.summary: A company, institution, agency, lab, school, collective, standards body, nonprofit, or group.

nodes.title: Person
nodes.summary: A human actor who created, influenced, witnessed, argued, documented, or participated in something.

nodes.title: Place
nodes.summary: A physical or virtual location where people, projects, organizations, or events exist or occurred.

nodes.title: Problem
nodes.summary: A difficulty, failure mode, design challenge, risk, or open need that organizes inquiry and work.

nodes.title: Project
nodes.summary: A built, proposed, fictional, abandoned, or active system with a purpose and lineage.

nodes.title: Publication
nodes.summary: A released intellectual or creative work such as a book, article, paper, film, episode, or talk.

nodes.title: Question
nodes.summary: An explicit inquiry, prompt, research question, or unresolved ask that motivates investigation and can be answered, partially answered, disputed, or left open.

nodes.title: Node Type Registry
nodes.summary: The canonical registry of current XanaNode node types, subtypes, visual treatment hints, and frontmatter recommendations.

nodes.title: Relationship
nodes.summary: A first-class edge treated as a node because the relationship itself has history, evidence, dispute, or explanation.

nodes.title: Response
nodes.summary: A reply, answer, rebuttal, clarification, or follow-up that responds to a question, communication, claim, or prior response.

nodes.title: Revision
nodes.summary: An immutable historical version of another node.

nodes.title: Schema
nodes.summary: A rule, ontology, validation document, or governance definition used by XanaNode itself.

nodes.title: Source
nodes.summary: A reference work, transcript, paper, book, talk, article, dataset, or primary evidence item.

nodes.title: Technology
nodes.summary: A tool, protocol, model, platform, architecture, method, or technical system.

nodes.title: Trail
nodes.summary: A curated route through the graph.

nodes.title: Birth date
nodes.summary: Calendar date on which a person, organization, project, or entity began.

nodes.title: Currency value
nodes.summary: A monetary amount with an ISO 4217 currency code.

nodes.title: External identifier
nodes.summary: A typed identifier from an external authority or registry.

nodes.title: Geographic coordinates
nodes.summary: WGS84 latitude and longitude coordinates for a place or geographically located entity.

nodes.title: SI measurement
nodes.summary: A quantified physical or conceptual measurement expressed in SI-compatible units.

nodes.title: Property Registry
nodes.summary: The canonical registry of standardized open-ended node properties for dates, coordinates, money, measurements, and external identifiers.

nodes.title: Property Registry Schema
nodes.summary: The JSON Schema for declaring standardized custom node properties such as measurements, coordinates, money values, identifiers, and dates.

nodes.title: Abstraction of
nodes.summary: The source abstracts away details from the target while preserving a relevant structure.

nodes.title: Alias of
nodes.summary: The source is an alternative name for the target entity.

nodes.title: Alternative to
nodes.summary: The source is an alternate interpretation, approach, or implementation relative to the target.

nodes.title: Analogous to
nodes.summary: The source is analogous to the target in a way that may aid reasoning or explanation.

nodes.title: Announced
nodes.summary: The source announced the target event, project, release, claim, or decision.

nodes.title: Announced By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The announced_by inverse relationship term derived from announced.

nodes.title: Answered By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The answered_by inverse relationship term derived from answers.

nodes.title: Answered in
nodes.summary: The source response, claim, or answer was given in the target communication context.

nodes.title: Answers
nodes.summary: The source answers the target question or information request.

nodes.title: Anticipated
nodes.summary: The source anticipated the target without necessarily directly causing it.

nodes.title: Anticipated By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The anticipated_by inverse relationship term derived from anticipated.

nodes.title: Anticipates
nodes.summary: The source foreshadows or historically precedes the target without direct causation.

nodes.title: Appears in
nodes.summary: The source appears in the target work, collection, or event.

nodes.title: Approved
nodes.summary: The source user, reviewer, or process approved the target.

nodes.title: Approved By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The approved_by inverse relationship term derived from approved.

nodes.title: Arrived From Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The arrived_from inverse relationship term derived from arrives_at.

nodes.title: Arrives at
nodes.summary: The source reasoning, argument, or inquiry leads to the target conclusion or position.

nodes.title: Asked
nodes.summary: The source asked, posed, or submitted the target question or request.

nodes.title: Asked By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The asked_by inverse relationship term derived from asked.

nodes.title: Asked in
nodes.summary: The source question was asked in the target communication context.

nodes.title: Attribution Required By Relationship Term

nodes.title: Authored
nodes.summary: The source authored the target.

nodes.title: Authored By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The authored_by inverse relationship term derived from authored.

nodes.title: Broadcast to
nodes.summary: The source communication, publication, event, or actor broadcast the target content or message to an audience.

nodes.title: Broader than
nodes.summary: The source schema type, concept, or relationship is broader or more general than the target.

nodes.title: Cited By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The cited_by inverse relationship term derived from cites.

nodes.title: Cites
nodes.summary: The source cites the target.

nodes.title: Clarified By Relationship Term

nodes.title: Clarifies
nodes.summary: The source clarifies, resolves ambiguity in, or makes the target more precise.

nodes.title: Coined

nodes.title: Coined By Relationship Term

nodes.title: Collaborated with

nodes.title: Communicated to

nodes.title: Communication Received From Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The communication_received_from inverse relationship term derived from communicated_to.

nodes.title: Compatible with
nodes.summary: The source schema, substrate, type, field, or relationship can interoperate with the target without destructive conversion.

nodes.title: Concrete Example Of Relationship Term

nodes.title: Contains
nodes.summary: The source contains the target.

nodes.title: Contemporary of

nodes.title: Context for
nodes.summary: The source provides essential background or framing that makes the target intelligible.

nodes.title: Continued From Relationship Term

nodes.title: Continues to

nodes.title: Contradicts
nodes.summary: The source directly challenges or conflicts with the target.

nodes.title: Contrasts with

nodes.title: Created
nodes.summary: The source created the target.

nodes.title: Created By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The created_by inverse relationship term derived from created.

nodes.title: Critical to

nodes.title: Debated with

nodes.title: Deep Linked From Relationship Term

nodes.title: Depicted By Relationship Term

nodes.title: Depicts

nodes.title: Derived from

nodes.title: Enables

nodes.title: Equivalent to

nodes.title: Evidence for

nodes.title: Example of
nodes.summary: The source is an example or concrete instance of the target.

nodes.title: Explained By Relationship Term

nodes.title: Explains

nodes.summary: The source extends the target.

nodes.summary: The source license, artifact, substrate, or policy requires attribution to the target person, organization, project, source, or community.

nodes.title: Requires context
nodes.summary: The source needs additional context to be interpreted responsibly.

nodes.title: Requires information
nodes.summary: The source cannot be resolved, accepted, or completed without additional information.

nodes.title: Requires review
nodes.summary: The source needs human, institutional, or automated review before it should be trusted or merged.

nodes.title: Requires source
nodes.summary: The source needs a cited source or primary evidence before it should be treated as supported.

nodes.title: Requires validation
nodes.summary: The source needs schema, evidence, reproducibility, or process validation.

nodes.title: Resonates with
nodes.summary: The source has a low-commitment conceptual or experiential resonance with the target.

nodes.title: Results from
nodes.summary: The source is a consequence, failure mode, output, or downstream effect of the target.

nodes.title: Review Required By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The review_required_by inverse relationship term derived from requires_review.

nodes.title: Reviewed Under Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The reviewed_under inverse relationship term derived from under_review.

nodes.title: Same concept
nodes.summary: The source and target represent the same concept across substrates, namespaces, versions, or local identifiers.

nodes.title: Same entity
nodes.summary: The source and target represent the same real-world entity across substrates, namespaces, versions, or local identifiers.

nodes.title: Same person
nodes.summary: The source and target represent the same person across substrates, namespaces, versions, or local identifiers.

nodes.title: Same source
nodes.summary: The source and target represent the same source or reference object across substrates, namespaces, versions, or local identifiers.

nodes.title: Shaped By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The shaped_by inverse relationship term derived from shapes.

nodes.title: Shapes
nodes.summary: The source significantly shapes or molds the form, direction, or character of the target.

nodes.title: Shares pattern with
nodes.summary: The source shares a recognizable structure, behavior, or pattern with the target.

nodes.title: Site Of Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The site_of inverse relationship term derived from occurred_at.

nodes.title: Source Required By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The source_required_by inverse relationship term derived from requires_source.

nodes.title: Specialized By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The specialized_by inverse relationship term derived from generalizes.

nodes.title: Specializes
nodes.summary: The source is a more specific case of the target.

nodes.title: Speculated By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The speculated_by inverse relationship term derived from speculative.

nodes.title: Speculative
nodes.summary: The source marks the target as speculative, provisional, or not yet established.

nodes.title: Spoke at
nodes.summary: The source person spoke at the target event.

nodes.title: Starts with
nodes.summary: The source sequence, work, or argument begins with the target.

nodes.title: Superseded By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The superseded_by inverse relationship term derived from supersedes.

nodes.title: Supersedes
nodes.summary: The source replaces the target.

nodes.title: Supported By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The supported_by inverse relationship term derived from supports.

nodes.title: Supports
nodes.summary: The source supports the target.

nodes.title: Tested By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The tested_by inverse relationship term derived from tests.

nodes.title: Tests
nodes.summary: The source tests the target hypothesis, claim, method, or prediction.

nodes.title: Threatened By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The threatened_by inverse relationship term derived from threatens.

nodes.title: Threatens
nodes.summary: The source threatens, endangers, or poses a risk to the target.

nodes.title: Trademark Holder For Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The trademark_holder_for inverse relationship term derived from trademarked_by.

nodes.title: trademarked by
nodes.summary: The source name, logo, brand asset, project identity, or mark is governed as a trademark by the target person, organization, project, or policy.

nodes.title: Trained on
nodes.summary: The source system or model was trained on the target dataset, corpus, or collection.

nodes.title: Transcluded By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The transcluded_by inverse relationship term derived from transcludes.

nodes.title: Transcludes
nodes.summary: The source includes a specific versioned fragment of the target by reference rather than copying it, preserving identity, attribution, and revision lineage.

nodes.title: Under review
nodes.summary: The source places the target under review or indicates that its status is being evaluated.

nodes.title: Unresolved
nodes.summary: The source marks the target as unresolved, unsettled, or not yet answerable.

nodes.title: Unresolved By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The unresolved_by inverse relationship term derived from unresolved.

nodes.title: Used As Primary Media For Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The used_as_primary_media_for inverse relationship term derived from has_primary_media.

nodes.title: Used By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The used_by inverse relationship term derived from uses.

nodes.title: Used To Train Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The used_to_train inverse relationship term derived from trained_on.

nodes.title: Uses
nodes.summary: The source uses, employs, or relies on the target.

nodes.title: Validated By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The validated_by inverse relationship term derived from validates.

nodes.title: Validates
nodes.summary: The source validates, confirms, or passes a test for the target hypothesis, claim, method, or artifact.

nodes.title: Validation Required By Relationship Term
nodes.summary: The validation_required_by inverse relationship term derived from requires_validation.

nodes.title: RO-Crate Metadata Schema
nodes.summary: The JSON Schema for Research Object Crate metadata used to describe packaged substrate artifacts and their provenance.

nodes.title: Substrate Diff Schema
nodes.summary: The JSON Schema for representing portable differences between substrate states.

nodes.title: Substrate Manifest Schema
nodes.summary: The JSON Schema defining substrate manifests, repository metadata, federation hints, imports, trust data, and pack metadata.

nodes.title: Substrate Node Schema
nodes.summary: The JSON Schema defining portable node records, including type, subtype, facets, media metadata, source snapshots, fragments, and relationships.

nodes.title: Substrate Relationships Schema
nodes.summary: The JSON Schema defining portable relationship records, including asserted time, validity time, confidence, evidence, and tumbler references.

nodes.title: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
nodes.summary: The Creative Commons license used for XanaNode protocol documentation, allowing sharing and adaptation with attribution.

nodes.title: CC0 1.0 Public Domain Dedication
nodes.summary: A public-domain dedication used when authors want to waive copyright restrictions as fully as possible.

nodes.title: GitHub Profile
nodes.summary: The public GitHub profile collecting XanaNode development activity and repositories.

nodes.title: GNU General Public License 3.0
nodes.summary: A strong copyleft open source license included as a comparative licensing concept.

nodes.title: Hugo Official Site
nodes.summary: The official website and documentation source for Hugo.

nodes.title: MIT License
nodes.summary: A widely used permissive open source license included as a comparative licensing concept.

nodes.title: XanaNode NOTICE

nodes.title: XanaNode.org
nodes.summary: A public XanaNode domain reserved for protocol, community, governance, or organizational presentation.

nodes.title: XanaNode Protocol Repository
nodes.summary: The public repository for the XanaNode protocol schemas, specifications, governance documents, registries, proposals, and examples.

nodes.title: XanaNode Studio Repository

nodes.title: XanaNode Trademark Policy
nodes.summary: The policy that governs use of the XanaNode name, logo, and related brand assets.

nodes.title: XanaNode Workspace Repository

nodes.title: Cytoscape.js
